name: pyreadr

metadata.name: pyreadr
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3 or later (AGPLv3+)
metadata.summary: Reads/writes R RData and Rds files into/from pandas data frames.

documentation:
A Python package to read and write R RData and Rds files into/from pandas data frames. It does not need to have R or other external dependencies installed. It is based on the C library librdata and a modified version of the cython wrapper jamovi-readstat.
Please visit out project home page for more information:
https://github.com/ofajardo/pyreadr

modules.name: tests.test_version
documentation: Test if the version in pyreadstat __init__ matches the version in setup.py

modules.name: setup
documentation: @author: Otto Fajardo

modules.name: pyreadr.pyreadr
documentation: @author: Otto Fajardo

functions.name: pyreadr.pyreadr.read_r
documentation:
Read an R RData or Rds file into pandas data frames

Parameters
----------
    path : str
        path to the file. The string is assumed to be utf-8 encoded.
    use_objects : list, optional
        a list with object names to read from the file. Only those objects will be imported. Case sensitive!
    timezone : str, optional
        timezone to localize datetimes, UTC otherwise.
        R datetimes (POSIXct and POSIXlt) are stored as UTC, but coverted to some timezone (explicitly if set by the
        user or implicitly to local zone) when displaying it in R. librdata cannot recover that timezone information
        therefore timestamps are displayed in UTC, unless this parameter is set.

Returns
-------
    result : OrderedDict
        object name as key and pandas data frame as value

functions.name: pyreadr.pyreadr.list_objects
documentation:
Read an R RData or Rds file and lists objects and their column names.
Not all objects are readable, and also it is not always possible to read the column names without parsing the
whole file, in those cases this method will return Nones instead of column names.

Parameters
----------
    path : str
        path to the file. The string is assumed to be utf-8 encoded.

Returns
-------
    result : list
        a list of dictionaries, where each dictionary has a key "object_name" with the name of the object and
        columns with a list of columns.

functions.name: pyreadr.pyreadr.write_rdata
documentation:
Write a single pandas data frame to a rdata file.

Parameters
----------
    path : str
        path to the file. The string is assumed to be utf-8 encoded.
    df : pandas data frame
        the dataframe to write
    df_name : str
        name for the R dataframe object, cannot be empty string. If 
        not supplied will default to "dataset"
    dateformat : str
        string to format datetime.date objects. 
        By default "%Y-%m-%d".
    datetimeformat : str
        string to format datetime like objects. By default "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S".

functions.name: pyreadr.pyreadr.write_rds
documentation:
Write a single pandas data frame to a rds file.

Parameters
----------
    path : str
        path to the file. The string is assumed to be utf-8 encoded.
    df : pandas data frame
        the dataframe to write
    dateformat : str
        string to format datetime.date objects. 
        By default "%Y-%m-%d".
    datetimeformat : str
        string to format datetime like objects. By default "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S".
    compress : str
        compression to use, defaults to no compression. Only "gzip" supported.

functions.name: pyreadr.pyreadr.download_file
documentation:
Downloads a file from a web url to destination_path.

Parameters
----------
url : str
    url of the file
destination_path : str
    path to write the file to disk

Returns
-------
str
    it gives back the path to where the file was written.

modules.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_writer
documentation: @author: Otto Fajardo

functions.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_writer.get_pyreadr_column_types
documentation:
From a pandas data frame, get an OrderedDict with column name as key
and pyreadr column type as value, and also a list with boolean 
values indicating if the column has missing values (np.nan).
The pyreadr column types are needed for downstream processing.

functions.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_writer.pyreadr_types_to_librdata_types
documentation: Transform pyreadr types to data types compatible with librdata

functions.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_writer.transform_data
documentation:
Get a column (pd.Series), pyreadr type (dtype) and boolean indicating
wheter there are missing values and transform the values to values
compatible with librdata.
dateformat and datetimeformat are strings used to format dates and
datetimes to strings.

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_writer.PyreadrWriter.compress_file
documentation: Compresses a file from src to dst with given compression

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_writer.PyreadrWriter.write_r
documentation:
write a RData or Rds file. 
path: str: path to the file
file_format: str: rdata or rds
df: pandas data frame
df_name = name of the object to write. Irrelevant if rds format.
dateformat: str: string to format dates
datetimeformat: str: string to format datetimes
compress: str: compression to use, for now only gzip supported.

classes.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table.convert_to_pandas_dataframe
documentation:
Coordinates all the necessary steps to convert the data collected from the parser to a pandas data frame.
\:return: a pandas data frame

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._arraylike_todf
documentation:
Method to convert objects with the dim attribute set: matrices, 
arrays and tables; also vectors if the user set the dim attribute
explicitly.
Internal methods
methods for arraylike: array, matrix, table

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._arraylike_convert
documentation: convert the data to suitable types

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._arraylike_buildf
documentation:
"
Transform the one dimensional array to a dataframe with several rows and columns

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table.arrange_dimnames_arraylike
documentation:
Dimenion names are captured as a flat array, transform to a nested array for
easier handling.

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._dflike_todf
documentation:
This one is for objects that do not have the dim attribute set: dataframes
and atomic vectors. (but vectors can have the dim attribute in that case
they go to the other method)
methods for data_frames
also vectors

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._consolidate_names
documentation: librdata may collect column (vector) names from two places, this method consolidates both.

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._todf
documentation: Converts the data to a pandas data frame, which still needs some further processing.

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._covert_data
documentation: Downstream processing of the data inside the pandas data frame

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._handle_row_names
documentation: For dataframes, set rownames as index

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.Table._handle_value_labels
documentation:
R factors are represented as integer vectors, and their string equivalences are stored somewhere else. This
method replaces the integers by the correspondent strings and transforms the data into a pandas category.
methods for both dim and no dim

In librdata each object is parsed into a "table".
This python object is passed to the Pyreadr parser and will collect all the data.
Once the parsing is finished it has methods to convert to pandas data frame.

classes.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_table
documentation:
Every object in the file is called table, this method is evoked once per object.
\:param name: str: the name of the table

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_column
documentation:
Evoked once per each column in the table.
\:param name: str: column name, may be None
\:param data_type: object of type DataType(Enum) (defined in librdata.pyx)
\:param data: a numpy array representing the data in R vector, may be empty
\:param count: int: number of elements in the array

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_column_name
documentation:
Some times name is None in handle column but it is recovered with this method.
\:param name: str: name of the column
\:param index: int: index of the column

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_dim
documentation:
Evoked once to retrieve the number of dimensions
\:param name: str: column name, may be None
\:param data_type: object of type DataType(Enum) (defined in librdata.pyx)
\:param data: a numpy array representing the number of dimensions
\:param count: int: number of elements in the array

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_dim_name
documentation:
Get one dimension name, one at a time, for matrices, arrays, tables.
\:param name: str: name of the dimension
\:param index: int: index of the dimension

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_row_name
documentation:
Handles R dataframe's rownames
\:param name: str: name of the row
\:param index: int: index of the row

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_text_value
documentation:
For character vectors this will be called once per row and will retrieve the string value for that row.
\:param name: str: string value for the row
\:param index: int: index of the row
\:return:

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.PyreadrParser.handle_value_label
documentation:
Factors are represented as integer vectors.
For factors, this method is called before reading the integer data in the Factor column with handle_column.
and will give all the string
values correspondent to the integer values.
\:param name: str: string value
\:param index: int: integer value
\:return:
Parses the RData or Rds file using the parser defined in librdata.pyx which in turn uses the C API of librdata.

methods.name: pyreadr._pyreadr_parser.ListObjectsParser.handle_column_name
